latest.title: Change of name and TIDM
latest.content:
Today 10:49
RNS Number : 5289O
Cykel AI PLC
12 January 2026
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
12 January 2026
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (formerly Cykel AI PLC)
(the "
Company
")
Change of name and TIDM
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (LSE: DFDV), a Main Market listed company which offers an AI agent platform that enables businesses to automate tasks through the use of specialised digital workers for
sales, recruitment and research and has adopted
a Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy,
is pleased to announce that the Company has changed its name and will trade under its new name DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC with effect from 12 January 2026.
In addition, with effect from 13 January 2026, the Company's TIDM will be amended from "CYK" to "DFDV" on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
The decision to change name to
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC
reflects the Company's continued development of its Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy as previously announced on 28 August 2025. The "Cykel" trading name will continue to be used for the Company's AI agent activities.
In addition, the Company's website address will also change to www.defidevcorp.co.uk.
Shareholders should note that their shareholdings will be unaffected by the change of name. Existing share certificates should be retained as they will remain valid for all purposes and no new share certificates will be issued.
The Company's ISIN and SEDOL will remain unchanged. The Company's Legal Entity Identifier remains 213800QEO6L6JAS62H02.
The Directors of the Company accept responsibility for this announcement.
*ENDS*
For further information:
Company
Michael Chan
Director
investors@defidevcorp.co.uk
First Sentinel (Corporate Adviser)
Brian Stockbridge

latest.title: Further re warrants
latest.content:
4 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 1455K
Cykel AI PLC
04 December 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
4 December 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("
Cykel AI
" or the "
Company
")
Further re Warrants
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces the following with respect to the warrants, full details of which are set out in the Company's announcement of 28 August 2025.
Warrants cancellation
The Company announces that it has agreed to the cancellation of 5,000,000 Pre-Paid and Investor Warrants (together, the "Warrants"). This will result in the repayment of the deposit already paid for the Pre-Paid Warrants (less a contribution to transaction costs) to the cancelling holders in accordance with the terms of the Warrants. Following this cancellation, the Company has 41,666,667 Warrants outstanding.
Warrants extension details
Under the existing terms, Warrants are not capable of being exercised until certain conditions which are detailed in the 28 August 2025 announcement have been satisfied and would have expired if such conditions were not satisfied by 30 November 2025. The Company and the remaining warrant holders, including DeFi Development Corp, have agreed to extend the period within which these conditions may be satisfied to 1 June 2026. Accordingly, the Warrants will only become exercisable once the relevant conditions have been fulfilled on or before 1 June 2026.
If the conditions have not been satisfied by 1 June 2026, the Warrants will expire and be cancelled and the deposit for the Pre-Paid Warrants will be repaid to the relevant investors (less pre
‑
agreed contributions to transaction costs).
All other terms of the Warrants remain unchanged.
Pre-Paid Warrants and Investor Warrants have the meanings given to them in the announcement published on 28 August 2025.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO
investors@cykel.ai
First Sentinel (Financial Adviser)

latest.title: Changes in Board Composition
latest.content:
3 Nov 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 7828F
Cykel AI PLC
03 November 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
3 November 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("Cykel AI" or the "Company")
Changes in Board Composition
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces that Nicholas Lyth has resigned as Interim Chairman of the Company, with immediate effect.
The Board would like to take this opportunity to thank Mr. Lyth for his contributions to the Company and wish him well for the future.
Michael Chan will assume the role of Chairman on an interim basis pending the appointment of a Non-executive Chairman in due course.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO
investors@cykel.ai
First Sentinel (Financial Adviser)

company_30s.what_they_do: Cykel AI builds and sells AI‑driven sales agents and marketing platforms for small‑to‑mid‑size businesses. Their flagship product, Eve, automates customer outreach and lead qualification, while GTM AI provides end‑to‑end campaign orchestration.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The AI‑sales space is expanding rapidly—global spend on sales automation is projected to hit $10 billion by 2030. Cykel AI’s low‑cost, plug‑and‑play solutions give mid‑market firms a chance to compete with the big SaaS players, creating a sizeable addressable market.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still pre‑revenue and heavily cash‑burning, having raised a £2.8 million round in August. Its stock has plunged from £2.00 to roughly 38 p in the last few months, leaving it in a deep‑crash position. The market is still waiting to see if the new product launches and funding rounds will turn the tide.

rns_narrative: Cykel AI has been busy announcing a series of product milestones—Eve and GTM AI in June and August—while simultaneously raising capital to keep the pipeline alive. The company’s latest interim results (Oct 2025) show modest revenue growth but still a negative cash flow, signalling a survival strategy rather than profitability.
The 12 Jan 2026 name change and TIDM filing suggests a re‑branding effort that could help reset investor perception, but it also indicates a need to clarify the company’s direction.

The August 2025 fundraise and the 27 Aug 2025 statement about share price movement hint that management is trying to shore up liquidity and calm a jittery market, which could be a sign of stabilization.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: A quiet, cautious hope—people are waiting for a clear signal before they commit.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders are pointing to the recent product launches and the upcoming name change as a potential catalyst. They believe the company’s AI tools could finally start generating revenue.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others warn that the company is still cash‑burning, the product roadmap is vague, and the stock’s price is still far below its pre‑crash levels. They fear another round of funding could dilute shareholders and that the market may never recover.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter is shifting from outright pessimism to a more measured optimism—people are still skeptical but are watching for a concrete earnings beat or a funding announcement.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat—no recent search spikes, meaning the market is largely unaware of the stock’s situation. This lack of noise is a green flag for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: The next interim report on 30 Oct 2025 could show whether the new AI products are starting to generate revenue. A positive earnings surprise would be a strong bounce trigger.
The 12 Jan 2026 change‑of‑name filing and any accompanying press release could reset the narrative and attract fresh investors.
A volume spike above 2 million shares traded on a single day—something that has happened on 8 Jan 2026—could signal institutional interest and a breakout from the current low.
Any announcement of a partnership or customer acquisition that puts the company on a realistic path to profitability would also serve as a catalyst.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden lift in trading volume (≥ 1.5 m shares) combined with a price break above the 38 p support level. Monitor the 30 Oct 2025 earnings release for any sign of revenue growth or a positive guidance revision.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock sits in the early phase of a deep‑crash cycle—capitulation seems complete, but the mean‑reversion engine hasn’t yet spun. The APEX score (tightly compressed) suggests the next move could be a sharp rebound if a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Cykel AI is 81 % below its pre‑crash price, putting it in the RED/ORANGE zone where the historical win rate is still respectable (≈ 40 %). If the stock recovers to a midway point of roughly 100 p, that’s a 164 % upside from the current 38 p level. The mean‑reversion thesis is backed by the company’s new AI offerings and recent funding, which could finally turn the cash burn into revenue generation.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company is still pre‑revenue and has a history of negative cash flow. If it fails to secure another funding round or the new products do not generate traction, the stock could fall further towards the 20 p support. A worst‑case scenario is a 70 % drop from the current price, wiping out the 38 p investment.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: a 21 % drop to 30 p (≈ 8 p). Reward: a 164 % gain to 100 p (≈ 62 p). The risk‑to‑reward ratio is roughly 1 : 7, which is highly attractive for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter a long position at the next support break (≈ 38 p) with a 10 % position size of your discretionary account. Place a stop 8 p below the entry (≈ 30 p) to limit loss to 21 %. Target 100 p and consider scaling out at 150 p if the stock shows sustained momentum.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume surge of at least 1.5 m shares and a price breakout above 38 p. If the 30 Oct 2025 earnings report shows positive revenue growth, consider adding to the position.
playbook.if_passing: None identified at present. The company is not facing an imminent delisting or bankruptcy filing.

ai_insights.bottom_line: CYK.L is a classic deep‑crash setup—81 % down, dead market chatter, and a potential catalyst in the next earnings release—offering a 7‑to‑1 risk‑reward upside if it rebounds to the midway point.
